violations.text: The floor in corners under/behind shelving in the dry storage room, behind the 3-door cooler, and behind the far end wall cabinets were observed to have droppings and debris. Pest control reports were reviewed. Per pest control, there is currently no new activity in the kitchen area. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean and to aid in the current pest control program. New Violation. Correct By: 03/18/2026

Bagged Ice was observed stored on the floor in the walk-in freezer. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Ice was picked up and stored off the floor at the time of inspections. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/28/2025

The floor in the corners behind the 3-door cooler and behind the far end wall cabinets were observed to have droppings and debris. Pest control reports were reviewed. Per pest control, there is currently no new activity in the kitchen area. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean and to aid in the current pest control program. Areas of concern were cleaned at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/28/2025
Refrigerator door gaskets at the 3 door refrigerator were observed in poor repair. Gaskets shall be repaired or replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 06/17/2025
Cans of pie filling were observed adulterated due to direct contact with rodent droppings. PIC voluntarily disposed of food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/23/2024

Italian wedding soup, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. PIC voluntarily disposed of food. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/23/2024

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by a dead mouse and rodents droppings observed on floor, shelves, and food product in dry storage area. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 11/06/2024

Broccoli and cheese soup, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. PIC voluntarily disposed of food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/07/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the shelving in the dry storage room have an accumulation of old rodent droppings. New Violation. Correct By: 03/07/2024

At the time of inspection, pelletized rodenticide was observed to be in use in an open container on the floor under a storage unit. Rodent bait shall be contained in a covered, tamper-resistant bait station. Maintenance personnel made aware at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 10/17/2023
The 3 Door Reach-In Refrigerator door gasket(s) were observed in poor repair. Equipment is to be maintained in good repair New Violation. Correct By: 11/17/2023

The drains for the ice machine and the sink used for food preparation is lacking a proper Air Gap (currently equipped with an Air Break). A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. New Violation. Correct By: 10/31/2023

The floor in the kitchen and storage areas were observed to have an accumulation of soil residue and rodent droppings behind and under equipment storage shelving. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean and to aid in monitoring of the pest control program. New Violation. Correct By: 10/21/2023
